iterations.theme: Bogatstvo naših neuspjeha: Put poniznosti, vjere i Božje providnosti

sections.subtitle: Uvodna molitva i pozdrav: 'Ja sam običan šljaker'
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić se predstavlja publici, naglašavajući svoju poniznost i običnost.
sections.content: Predavanje je započelo uvodnom molitvom koju je predvodio **Zlatko Đimorić**, pozivajući prisutne na zajedničku molitvu i osmijeh. Naglasio je važnost radosti Evanđelja i zazvao Gospodina da bude prisutan i promijeni srca slušatelja, kako je to ranije spomenuo i fra Mario. Zlatko se predstavio kao 'običan šljaker, radnik, crnac, šofer', ističući da je sve što jest, po milosti Božjoj. Njegova iskrenost i poniznost odmah su postavile ton predavanja, naglašavajući da je Bog taj koji ga vodi i osposobljava.
sections.subtitle_en: Introductory prayer and greeting: 'I am an ordinary worker'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić introduces himself to the audience, emphasizing his humility and ordinariness.
sections.content_en: The lecture began with an opening prayer led by **Zlatko Đimorić**, inviting those present to common prayer and a smile. He emphasized the importance of the joy of the Gospel and called upon the Lord to be present and change the hearts of the listeners, as fra Mario had mentioned earlier. Zlatko introduced himself as an 'ordinary laborer, worker, drudge, driver', emphasizing that everything he is, is by the grace of God. His sincerity and humility immediately set the tone of the lecture, emphasizing that God is the one who leads and enables him.

sections.subtitle: Zahvalnost zajednici i nesebičnom Krunoslavu Puski
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić zahvaljuje Krunoslavu Puski i publici, uz pljesak prisutnih.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je izrazio duboku zahvalnost zajednici, posebno ističući molitvu krunice ispred zagrebačke katedrale, koju je opisao kao veliku potrebu i milost za sve očeve i muževe. Posebnu zahvalnost uputio je **Krunoslavu Puski**, organizatoru događaja, koji je 'bio toliko lud da je ostavio posao u školi i potpuno se posvetio' okupljanju katoličkih muževa. Đimorić je naglasio da je Duh Sveti taj koji je potaknuo sve prisutne i njega samog da se okupe i budu na slavu Božju, pozivajući na pljesak Duhu Svetom.
sections.subtitle_en: Gratitude to the community and the selfless Krunoslav Puski
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić thanks Krunoslav Puski and the audience, with the applause of those present.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić expressed deep gratitude to the community, especially highlighting the prayer of the Rosary in front of the Zagreb cathedral, which he described as a great need and grace for all fathers and husbands. He sent special gratitude to **Krunoslav Puska**, the organizer of the event, who 'was so crazy that he left his job at the school and completely dedicated himself' to the gathering of Catholic husbands. Đimorić emphasized that it is the Holy Spirit who prompted all those present and himself to gather and be for the glory of God, calling for applause for the Holy Spirit.

sections.subtitle: Bogatstvo naših neuspjeha: Božja poruka u kamionu-kapelici
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić objašnjava kako mu je Bog progovorio o temi 'bogatstva neuspjeha' dok je vozio kamion.
sections.content: Nakon početne panike zbog poziva da bude gost predavač, Zlatko Đimorić je od Boga primio jasnu temu: **'Bogatstvo naših neuspjeha'**. Objasnio je kako je njegov kamion postao 'kapelica', mjesto susreta s Gospodinom, gdje se fokusira isključivo na Božju riječ, ignorirajući svjetovne vijesti. Upitao je publiku tko je doživio razočaranje ili neuspjeh, a većina je podigla ruke, potvrđujući univerzalnost teme. Istaknuo je da Crkva nije zajednica savršenih, već običnih ljudi koji se suočavaju s izazovima, za razliku od 'savršenih' likova iz Hollywooda koji često završavaju na antidepresivima.
sections.subtitle_en: The wealth of our failures: God's message in the truck-chapel
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić explains how God spoke to him about the theme of 'the wealth of failure' while he was driving a truck.
sections.content_en: After the initial panic because of the invitation to be a guest lecturer, Zlatko Đimorić received a clear theme from God: **'The Wealth of Our Failures'**. He explained how his truck became a 'chapel', a place of encounter with the Lord, where he focuses exclusively on God's word, ignoring worldly news. He asked the audience who had experienced disappointment or failure, and the majority raised their hands, confirming the universality of the theme. He pointed out that the Church is not a community of the perfect, but of ordinary people who face challenges, unlike the 'perfect' characters from Hollywood who often end up on antidepressants.

sections.subtitle: Poniznost kao preduvjet Božje slave: Biblijski primjeri
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić govori o biblijskim primjerima poniznosti, poput Kralja Davida.
sections.content:
Zlatko Đimorić je naglasio da **poniznost uvijek prethodi Božjoj slavi**. Kao primjere je naveo biblijske likove:
      - **Blaženu Djevicu Mariju**, poniznu službenicu Božju.
      - **Poniznog Indijanca iz Guadalupe**, koji je milijune preobratio.
      - **Kralja Davida**, kojeg je Bog izabrao unatoč tome što ga je vlastiti otac smatrao nevažnim.
      - **Josipa Egipatskog**, koji je prošao kroz poniženje i tamnicu prije nego što je bio spreman za Božju slavu, shvaćajući da 'ja ništa ne znam, ali onaj kojemu služim zna sve'.
sections.subtitle_en: Humility as a precondition for God's glory: Biblical examples
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić speaks about biblical examples of humility, such as King David.
sections.content_en:
Zlatko Đimorić emphasized that **humility always precedes God's glory**. As examples he cited biblical figures:
      - **Blessed Virgin Mary**, humble servant of God.
      - **Humble Indian from Guadalupe**, who converted millions.
      - **King David**, whom God chose despite the fact that his own father considered him unimportant.
      - **Joseph of Egypt**, who passed through humiliation and prison before he was ready for God's glory, realizing that 'I know nothing, but the one whom I serve knows everything'.

sections.subtitle: Bog djeluje kroz 'nesposobne': Sveti Franjo i Arški župnik
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić priča o Arškom župniku i njegovoj 'nesposobnosti' koja je postala put do svetosti.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je nastavio s primjerima kako Bog djeluje kroz one koje svijet smatra 'nesposobnima'. Spomenuo je **Svetog Franju**, čiji je red papa odobrio tek nakon vizije o siromašnom čovjeku koji drži Crkvu. Posebno je istaknuo **Arškog župnika**, kojeg su biskupi smatrali toliko nesposobnim da su ga poslali u zapuštenu crkvu u Arsu. No, župnik se potpuno posvetio molitvi, postu i Presvetom, privlačeći vjernike iz cijele Francuske i na kraju je proglašen svecem. Đimorić je zaključio da Bog započinje dobro djelo rođenjem svakog čovjeka i želi ga dovršiti, ali na svoj, a ne na ljudski način.
sections.subtitle_en: God works through 'the incapable': Saint Francis and the Curé of Ars
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić talks about the Curé of Ars and his 'incapacity' which became a path to holiness.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić continued with examples of how God acts through those whom the world considers 'incapable'. He mentioned **Saint Francis**, whose order the pope approved only after a vision of a poor man holding the Church. He especially highlighted the **Arški župnik**, whom the bishops considered so incapable that they sent him to a neglected church in Ars. However, the parish priest completely devoted himself to prayer, fasting, and the Blessed Sacrament, attracting believers from all over Francuska and in the end was declared a saint. Đimorić concluded that God begins a good work with the birth of every person and wants to complete it, but in His own way, and not in a human way.

sections.subtitle: Pustinja života: Mjesto milosti i susreta s Bogom
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić objašnjava kako su teški trenuci zapravo 'pustinja života' gdje se susreće s Bogom.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je objasnio da su 'pustinje života' – neuspjesi, razočaranja i patnje – zapravo **mjesta milosti**. U tim trenucima, kada nema 'sjaja i blještavila', čovjek se fokusira na bitno: odnos s Bogom. Prisjetio se vlastitog iskustva kada je bio 'zgažen do kraja', osjećajući se kao 'najveće smeće', a tada mu je Bog progovorio: 'Ne boj se, ja sam s tobom.' Istaknuo je da Bog ne lomi napuklu slamku i ne gasi vatru koja tinja, već nas želi podići za vječnost. Priznati slabost nije tragedija, već poziv na oslanjanje na Božji pogon, dopuštajući Duhu Svetom da nas pokrene na nadnaravno.
sections.subtitle_en: The desert of life: A place of grace and encounter with God
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić explains how difficult moments are actually the 'desert of life' where he meets with God.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić explained that 'deserts of life' – failures, disappointments, and sufferings – are actually **places of grace**. In those moments, when there is no 'glitter and glamour', a person focuses on the essential: the relationship with God. He recalled his own experience when he was 'crushed to the end', feeling like the 'greatest trash', and then God spoke to him: 'Do not be afraid, I am with you.' He pointed out that God does not break a bruised reed and does not extinguish a smoldering fire, but wants to lift us up for eternity. Admitting weakness is not a tragedy, but a call to rely on God's drive, allowing the Holy Spirit to move us toward the supernatural.

sections.subtitle: Osobna kriza i prekretnica: Invaliditet, dugovi i vjera supruge
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić opisuje trenutak u bolnici kada mu je supruga objavila trudnoću, što je bila prekretnica.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je podijelio svoje duboko osobno svjedočanstvo o krizi koja ga je zadesila. Izgubio je sav novac u apartmanima, bio je u ogromnim dugovima zbog kredita u švicarskim francima, a prometna nesreća ga je učinila invalidom. U tom najnižem trenutku, kada je mislio da je 'gotov', njegova supruga **Tereshina** donijela je svjetlo vjere i nade. U bolnici mu je otkrila da je trudna s njihovim petim djetetom, izjavivši: 'Zlatko, ja neću pokleknuti... Bog ima plan za nas.' Te riječi su bile prekretnica. Bolnički kapelan mu je tada proročki rekao da ga Bog zove da mu preda život i da će pisati o Njemu, što je Zlatko, unatoč svojoj 'nepismenosti', prihvatio.
sections.subtitle_en: Personal crisis and turning point: Disability, debts, and the wife's faith
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić describes the moment in the hospital when his wife announced the pregnancy to him, which was a turning point.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić shared his deeply personal testimony about the crisis that befell him. He lost all the money in apartments, he was in huge debts because of loans in Swiss francs, and a traffic accident made him disabled. In that lowest moment, when he thought he was 'finished', his wife **Tereshina** brought the light of faith and hope. In the hospital she revealed to him that she was pregnant with their fifth child, stating: 'Zlatko, I will not yield... God has a plan for us.' Those words were a turning point. The hospital chaplain then prophetically told him that God was calling him to surrender his life to Him and that he would write about Him, which Zlatko, despite his 'illiteracy', accepted.

sections.subtitle: Božja providnost u izdavanju knjige: Od odbijanja do Radio Marije
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić priča kako je Radio Marija čitala njegovu knjigu, unatoč početnim odbijanjima.
sections.content: Nakon što je napisao knjigu, Zlatko Đimorić se suočio s nizom odbijanja – od vlastite župe do kršćanskih knjižara poput **Verbuma** i **Kršćanske sadašnjosti**, te **Katoličkog radija**. Osjećajući se napušteno, čuo je Božji glas koji ga je podsjetio da se fokusira na milost, a ne na uloženi novac. Nakon toga, dogodila su se čuda: knjižare su prihvatile knjigu, Katolički radio mu je ponudio besplatnu promociju, a **Radio Marija** je čak čitala njegovu knjigu u eteru, a da on to nije ni znao. Zlatko je humoristično spomenuo i 'mučeništvo' **Krunoslava Puske** koji je lektorirao njegovu knjigu, ističući da se Bog pobrinuo za promociju na svoj način.
sections.subtitle_en: God's providence in the publishing of the book: From rejection to Radio Marija
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić tells how Radio Marija read his book, despite initial rejections.
sections.content_en: After he wrote the book, Zlatko Đimorić faced a series of rejections – from his own parish to Christian bookstores like **Verbum** and **Kršćanska sadašnjost**, and **Katolički radio**. Feeling abandoned, he heard God's voice which reminded him to focus on grace, and not on the money invested. After that, miracles happened: bookstores accepted the book, Katolički radio offered him free promotion, and **Radio Marija** even read his book on air, without him even knowing it. Zlatko humorously mentioned also the 'martyrdom' of **Krunoslav Puska** who proofread his book, emphasizing that God took care of the promotion in His own way.

sections.subtitle: Prevladavanje straha i prihvaćanje nesavršenosti: 'Nisam mister savršen'
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić govori o prevladavanju straha od neuspjeha i prihvaćanju svojih mana.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je podijelio kako ga je Gospodin kroz poniznost i neuspjeh osposobio za govorništvo. Prisjetio se svojih problema s tremom u brzom klizanju (SPID), gdje je na utrkama podbacio unatoč dobrim treninzima. Shvatio je da ne mora biti savršen, već da smije pogriješiti. Počeo je vježbati 'kako biti zadnji' na treningu, oslobađajući se pritiska savršenstva. Otvoreno je priznao svoju lošu školsku prošlost – tri razreda za vozača, ponavljanje razreda – što je nekada tajio. Sada to s ponosom dijeli, naglašavajući da Bogu nisu zanimljivi profesionalci, već 'amateri' koji rade iz ljubavi.
sections.subtitle_en: Overcoming fear and accepting imperfection: 'I am not Mr. Perfect'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić speaks about overcoming the fear of failure and accepting one's flaws.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić shared how the Lord, through humility and failure, equipped him for public speaking. He recalled his problems with stage fright in speed skating (SPID), where he failed in races despite good training sessions. He realized that he does not have to be perfect, but that he is allowed to make a mistake. He began practicing 'how to be last' at training, freeing himself from the pressure of perfection. He openly admitted his poor school past – three grades for a driver, repeating a grade – which he once kept secret. Now he shares this with pride, emphasizing that professionals are not interesting to God, but 'amateurs' who work out of love.

sections.subtitle: Božja providnost u financijskim poteškoćama: Priča o šumi
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić priča o tome kako je dobio šumu u Delnicama, unatoč financijskim problemima.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je svjedočio o nevjerojatnoj Božjoj providnosti u svojim financijskim poteškoćama. Kada je bio u velikim dugovima i mislio da nikada neće imati vlastitu kuću, Bog mu je progovorio da će je imati. Podijelio je i priču o podjeli obiteljske imovine s tri sestre i posvojenim bratom **Martinom**. Nesebično je prepustio svoj dio obiteljske kuće i šumu sestrama. Kasnije, kada je bio potpuno bez novca, Bog mu je omogućio da kupi veliku šumu u **Delnicama** za simboličan iznos, a novac za kupnju i porez je zaradio sječom drva. To je bio jasan znak da Bog ne zaboravlja velikodušnost srca.
sections.subtitle_en: God's providence in financial difficulties: The story of the forest
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić talks about how he received a forest in Delnice, despite financial problems.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić testified about incredible God's providence in his financial difficulties. When he was in great debts and thought that he would never have his own house, God spoke to him that he would have it. He also shared a story about the division of family property with three sisters and adopted brother **Martinom**. Unselfishly he left his part of the family house and the forest to the sisters. Later, when he was completely without money, God enabled him to buy a large forest in **Delnicama** for a symbolic amount, and the money for the purchase and tax he earned by cutting wood. That was a clear sign that God does not forget the generosity of heart.

sections.subtitle: Božja nagrada za velikodušnost: Priča o Mercedesu
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić priča o tome kako mu je otac-punac poklonio Mercedes nakon čina velikodušnosti.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je ispričao još jednu priču o Božjoj nagradi za velikodušnost. Odlučio je kupiti stari **Mercedes** od svog tasta za 20.000 kuna. Nakon što je odlučio dati desetinu i dodatnih 5.000 kuna za Božić, preplavila ga je neopisiva radost tijekom svete mise. Po povratku kući, tast mu je rekao da mu ne može prodati automobil, već mu ga želi pokloniti, vraćajući mu 20.000 kuna. Zlatko je to protumačio kao izravnu nagradu od Isusa, koji 'vidi što radimo i s kojom nakanom srca'.
sections.subtitle_en: God's reward for generosity: The story about the Mercedes
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić talks about how his father-in-law gifted him a Mercedes after an act of generosity.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić told another story about God's reward for generosity. He decided to buy an old **Mercedes** from his father-in-law for 20,000 kuna. After he decided to give a tithe and an additional 5,000 kuna for Christmas, an indescribable joy overwhelmed him during the Holy Mass. Upon returning home, his father-in-law told him that he could not sell him the car, but instead wanted to give it to him as a gift, returning the 20,000 kuna to him. Zlatko interpreted this as a direct reward from Jesus, who 'sees what we do and with what intention of the heart'.

sections.subtitle: Bit braka: Zahvalnost, služenje i opraštanje
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić objašnjava tri ključna stupa braka: zahvalnost, služenje i opraštanje.
sections.content:
Razmišljajući o braku, Zlatko Đimorić je, inspiriran vjenčanjem kojem je prisustvovao, istaknuo tri ključna stupa:
      - **Zahvalnost**: 'Zahvalnost je govor ljubavi.' Naglasio je važnost zahvaljivanja supružniku za svakodnevne stvari.
      - **Služenje**: 'U služenju se otkriva veličina vaše ljubavi.' Pozvao je na davanje, a ne na primanje.
      - **Opraštanje**: 'U opraštanju je ključ vaše neraskidivosti.' Bez opraštanja, brak ne može opstati.
      Ove riječi, kako je rekao, donose veliku mudrost za sve bračne parove.
sections.subtitle_en: The essence of marriage: Gratitude, service, and forgiveness
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić explains the three key pillars of marriage: gratitude, service, and forgiveness.
sections.content_en:
Thinking about marriage, Zlatko Đimorić, inspired by a wedding he attended, highlighted three key pillars:
      - **Gratitude**: 'Gratitude is the speech of love.' He emphasized the importance of thanking the spouse for everyday things.
      - **Service**: 'In service, the greatness of your love is revealed.' He called for giving, not receiving.
      - **Forgiveness**: 'In forgiveness is the key to your indissolubility.' Without forgiveness, marriage cannot survive.
      These words, as he said, bring great wisdom for all married couples.

sections.subtitle: Preoblikovanje invaliditeta: Od mane do prednosti
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić humoristično pokazuje svoju protezu i objašnjava kako je to prednost.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je s humorom i dubokim uvidom progovorio o svom invaliditetu i protezi. Umjesto da je vidi kao manu, on je preoblikovao u prednost: 'Imam besplatan parking u prvim redovima', 'duplo manje nožnih noktiju moram rezati', 'ova se uopće ne znoji'. Postavio je pitanje publici: 'Je li ovo mana ili je ovo prednost?' Naglasio je da je ključ u perspektivi – gledati svijet pozitivno i fokusirati se na Božje svjetlo. Takav pogled omogućuje da se iz neuspjeha izađe kao pobjednik, pretvarajući ono što se čini nedostatkom u snagu.
sections.subtitle_en: Reshaping disability: From a flaw to an advantage
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić humorously shows his prosthesis and explains how that is an advantage.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić spoke with humor and deep insight about his disability and prosthesis. Instead of seeing it as a flaw, he reshaped it into an advantage: 'I have free parking in the front rows', 'I have to cut half as many toenails', 'this one doesn't sweat at all'. He asked the audience a question: 'Is this a flaw or is this an advantage?' He emphasized that the key is in perspective – looking at the world positively and focusing on God's light. Such a view enables one to emerge from failure as a winner, turning what seems like a deficiency into strength.

sections.subtitle: Naslovnica knjige i univerzalni poziv na svjedočenje
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić objašnjava simboliku naslovnice svoje knjige 'Ti si svjedok'.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je objasnio simboliku naslovnice svoje knjige. Na njoj se nalazi silueta čovjeka s protezom, koji trči iz tame života prema svjetlu, prema nebu, ne šepajući i ne predajući se. Poruka 'Moj si plač u igru krenuo' preuzeta je iz **Psalma 30** i predstavlja srž njegove knjige. Glavni naslov, **'Ti si svjedok'**, nije namijenjen samo njemu, već svakom pojedincu, jer je 'vaš život jedno evanđelje koje će netko pročitati na vašem radnom mjestu, školi, fakultetu'. To je univerzalni poziv na svjedočenje vlastitim životom.
sections.subtitle_en: Book cover and the universal call to witnessing
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić explains the symbolism of the cover of his book 'You are a witness'.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić explained the symbolism of the cover of his book. On it is a silhouette of a man with a prosthesis, who runs from the darkness of life toward the light, toward heaven, not limping and not giving up. The message 'You turned my weeping into a dance' is taken from **Psalm 30** and represents the core of his book. The main title, **'You are a witness'**, is not intended only for him, but for every individual, because 'your life is one gospel that someone will read at your workplace, school, college'. This is a universal call to witnessing with one's own life.

sections.subtitle: Završna molitva i radikalna poniznost: 'Poljubi mu noge'
sections.screenshot_description: Zlatko Đimorić završava predavanje molitvom i snažnom porukom o poniznosti.
sections.content: Zlatko Đimorić je zaključio predavanje završnom molitvom, zahvaljujući Bogu na susretu i pozivu na ljubav, te moleći za milost da budu očevi i muževi kakve je Bog zamislio. Podijelio je i snažnu poruku o radikalnoj poniznosti: kada je imao nesporazum s prijateljem, Isus mu je rekao da mu 'poljubi noge'. Zlatko je to učinio iz ljubavi Kristove, naglašavajući da nas Bog poziva na služenje Kristu, Crkvi, obitelji i svima, kako bismo bili 'vojska bezgrešna u poniznosti'. Na kraju je spomenuo da sav prihod od prodaje njegove knjige ide za zakladu **Krunoslava Puske**, zahvaljujući mu na svemu.
sections.subtitle_en: Closing prayer and radical humility: 'Kiss his feet'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Zlatko Đimorić finishes the lecture with a prayer and a powerful message about humility.
sections.content_en: Zlatko Đimorić concluded the lecture with a closing prayer, thanking God for the meeting and the call to love, and praying for the grace to be fathers and husbands as God intended. He also shared a powerful message about radical humility: when he had a misunderstanding with a friend, Jesus told him to 'kiss his feet'. Zlatko did this out of the love of Christ, emphasizing that God calls us to serve Christ, the Church, family, and everyone, so that we would be an 'immaculate army in humility'. At the end, he mentioned that all proceeds from the sale of his book go to the foundation of **Krunoslav Puska**, thanking him for everything.

iterations.theme_en: The wealth of our failures: The path of humility, faith, and God's providence
